name: Grilled Chicken Breasts with Mango Salsa
recipeInstructions:
Cucumbers should be finely diced, unpeeled.
Trim the chicken breast halves of all fat and gristle and separate the tenderloin. Mix soy, oil, honey, lime juice, salt, and cayenne in a shallow dish large enough to hold the chicken in one close layer. Marinate the chicken for 1 hour at room temperature or several hours in the refrigerator, turning several times. Grill the chicken breasts for 3 to 4 minutes per side, and tenderloins about 2 minutes per side. The chicken may also be sautéed over moderate heat in a skillet lightly filmed with oil. The bottom of the skillet will darken as the honey caramelizes but is easily cleaned.
Cut the breasts into thick slices and fan on dinner plates, placing the tenderloins alongside. Spoon the salsa over the top and drizzle with the mayonnaise.
MANGO SALSA
Slice the mango flesh from each side of the pit. One half at a time, make an inch cut just inside the skin, then score the flesh in 3/8-inch cubes. Turn the skin inside out and free the mango flesh with the knife. Cut the cubes into 1-inch dice, reserving any mushy pieces for another use. You should end up with about 1 1/2 cups. Combine mango with all remaining ingredients and let sit 30 minutes to blend flavors.
Note: 1 1/2 Cups peach or papaya diced may be substituted for the mango.
